title: 10 best herbal essential oils for tinnitus
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for tinnitus are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate ringing, buzzing, or other sounds associated with tinnitus. These oils work by reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality, which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms. The best herbal essential oils for tinnitus include Zingiber officinale, Lavandula angustifolia, Melissa officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Rosmarinus officinalis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, and Cinnamomum verum. If left untreated, tinnitus can severely impact daily life, leading to anxiety, depression, and social isolation, ultimately affecting relationships, work, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with tinnitus because of its calming and relaxing properties, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that often exacerbates the condition. The oil's soothing aroma can also promote a sense of tranquility, allowing individuals to better cope with the constant ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus. Additionally, lavender's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties may help to alleviate the discomfort and pain sometimes experienced by tinnitus sufferers, providing relief from this often debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: English Lavender essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that can exacerbate Tinnitus symptoms, allowing for better sleep and reduced tinnitus distress.

Anti-inflammatory: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate Tinnitus-related inflammation in the inner ear, which is often associated with this condition, thus reducing the ringing or buzzing sensations.

Antioxidant: English Lavender essential oil is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the cells of the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to tinnitus development and progression, thereby promoting overall ear health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in alleviating tinnitus symptoms.

Flowers: Lavender flowers are rich in linalool and linalyl acetate, two main compounds responsible for their calming effects on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress associated with tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at dawn and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Steam distill the lavender flowers in a clevenger apparatus for 3 hours using 100g of flowers per 500ml of water.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from the hydrosol by decantation or filtering through diatomaceous earth.
Transfer the essential oil to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dry place for 24 hours before use.
Blend 10% lavender essential oil with 90% sweet almond oil or jojoba oil as a carrier oil for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with tinnitus because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that often exacerbate this condition. The oil's natural relaxant effects can also improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted in individuals with tinnitus. Furthermore, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the inner ear, a common underlying cause of tinnitus. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, lemon balm essential oils can provide relief from the constant ringing or buzzing associated with tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to tinnitus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm essential oil may reduce inflammation in the auditory system, which is thought to contribute to tinnitus symptoms such as ringing or buzzing in the ears.
Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Lemon balm essential oil's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for tinnitus episodes and can worsen symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain a high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are rich in citral, a natural compound that has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, common comorbidities with tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Wash the harvested material gently with clean water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the washed material using 1 part plant material to 4 parts water for 2 hours.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separating funnel.
Bottle the extracted essential oil in dark glass containers at room temperature after filtering through coffee filters.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with tinnitus because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that often exacerbates the condition. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also contribute to its effectiveness in alleviating tinnitus symptoms by reducing inflammation in the ear canal and eardrum, which can cause ringing or buzzing sensations. Additionally, chamomile's sedative effects can promote relaxation, helping to calm the mind and body, further diminishing tinnitus perceptions. By incorporating chamomile essential oils into one's daily routine, individuals with tinnitus may experience significant relief from their symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the ears, which is often a contributing factor to tinnitus.
Antioxidant: As an antioxidant, chamomile essential oil helps protect the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to tinnitus.

Anxiolytic: Chamomile essential oil has a calming and soothing effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety related to tinnitus, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them effective for soothing tinnitus symptoms.
Roots: Contains apiumin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort associated with tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh chamomile flowers at dawn when dew is still present ensuring optimal oil content.
Steam distill the harvested flowers using 10% of water and 90% steam for 3 hours yielding 1%.
Filter the obtained essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight fitting cap at room temperature for 24 hours.
Blend the stored essential oil with a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil at a ratio of 3% matricaria chamomilla to 97% carrier.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with tinnitus because of its unique ability to calm and soothe the auditory system. The oil's potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ears, which are common underlying causes of tinnitus. Additionally, its calming effects on the nervous system can help quiet the ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus, promoting a sense of peace and tranquility. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maidenhair Tree essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the ear canal and auditory system, which is often linked to tinnitus.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds in Maidenhair Tree essential oil can help protect the inner ear cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to tinnitus symptoms.
Nervine-calming: The nervine-calming properties of Maidenhair Tree essential oil may help soothe and calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety, stress, and hyperactivity that can exacerbate tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Ginkgo biloba roots have been traditionally used to treat various ear disorders, including tinnitus, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Barks: The barks of the Maidenhair Tree contain compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids that have been shown to improve blood flow to the inner ear and reduce oxidative stress, making it effective for treating tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reputable supplier.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a warm and dark place for 24 hours.
Use a food processor to chop the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces weighing 100 grams.
Combine the chopped ginkgo biloba with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place before straining and filtering the essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower essential oils helps with tinnitus because of its unique composition, which includes anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation in the ear canal can help alleviate the symptoms of tinnitus, such as ringing or buzzing sounds. Additionally, the antioxidants present in the oil may help to protect the hair cells in the cochlea from damage, further contributing to relief from tinnitus. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Echinacea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cichoric acid and cynarin, may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which could contribute to tinnitus.
Immune system modulation: Echinacea has been shown to modulate the immune system, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the ear.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds present in Echinacea, such as quercetin and kaempferol, may help protect the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, which could contribute to tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, the roots are a good source of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that can help soothe tinnitus symptoms.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them effective for tinnitus relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea angustifolia flowers in the early morning mist when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 95°f for 24 hours or air dry them for 7-10 days.
Blend 50g of dried echinacea angustifolia flowers with 1 liter of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil and let it sit for 2 weeks.
Shake the mixture daily and filter out the plant material after 14 days to obtain the infused oil.
Steam distill the infused oil using a clevenger apparatus at atmospheric pressure for 3 hours to produce echinacea angustifolia essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with tinnitus because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the irritation and inflammation caused by tinnitus. The oil's potent antioxidants also work to combat oxidative stress in the ears, alleviating the ringing or buzzing sensations associated with the condition. Additionally, pot marigold's calming effects on the nervous system may help regulate auditory function and promote a sense of inner peace, further mitigating the distressing effects of tinnitus.

Antioxidant: Neutralizes free radicals that can damage hair cells and nerve endings in the inner ear, potentially contributing to tinnitus, while also protecting against oxidative stress.

Flowers: The flowers of pot marigold are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that could potentially benefit individuals suffering from tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature calendula officinalis flowers, carefully picking them at dawn to ensure optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture levels.
Grind the dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, resulting in approximately 100 grams of material.
Combine the powdered calendula with a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil at a 1:2 ratio, stirring well to ensure uniform mixture.
Steam distill the calendula-carrier oil mixture for 2-3 hours, collecting the resulting essential oils in a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop essential oils helps with tinnitus because its unique blend of compounds, including sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, has a profound impact on the auditory system. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which is often associated with tinnitus. Additionally, maypop's ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels can also contribute to its effectiveness in managing tinnitus symptoms. Barks: The barks of the Maypop plant contain compounds that help to soothe and calm the auditory system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passionflower leaves and flowers and dry them for at least 24 hours in a warm, well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried passionflower material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine one part of the powdered passionflower material with two parts of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a dark glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids, then transfer the passiflora incarnata essential oil to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with tinnitus because of its natural sedative properties, which can help to calm the nerves and reduce anxiety. Tinnitus is often linked to emotional stress, so by promoting relaxation and reducing tension, valerian oil may help to alleviate symptoms. Additionally, valerian's analgesic properties can also help to numb or mask ringing in the ears, providing temporary relief from tinnitus. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after the dew has dried.
Wash the harvested roots gently with lukewarm water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the washed roots using a clevenger apparatus for 30-40 minutes.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distilled water using a separating funnel at 1:5 dilution ratio.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment within 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose essential oils helps with tinnitus because of its unique ability to calm the nervous system and reduce stress and anxiety. The oil's natural sedative properties can help to quiet a racing mind, allowing individuals to relax and focus on their surroundings, rather than being consumed by the constant ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus. Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of damask rose essential oil help protect the ears from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of tinnitus and other hearing-related issues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh rosa damascena flowers at peak blooming season.
Steam distill the flowers in a still using 500 ml of distilled water for 3 hours.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distillate using a hydrosol separator.
Mix 10 ml of the collected essential oil with 90 ml of jojoba oil as a carrier oil.
Store the blended oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature for 2 weeks to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with tinnitus because its calming and sedative properties can reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, thereby alleviating ringing or buzzing sensations in the ears. The antiseptic and antibacterial qualities of thyme oil may also help to heal any underlying infections or irritations that could be contributing to tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, thyme's analgesic properties can provide relief from the discomfort and stress associated with chronic ear conditions. By promoting a sense of calm and reducing inflammation, thyme essential oils may offer a natural solution for managing tinnitus symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the auditory system, which can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme essential oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat infections and promote a healthy inner ear environment, potentially reducing tinnitus symptoms caused by middle ear infections or other bacterial/viral causes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves at dawn on a sunny day when they are still slightly dewy.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area away from direct sunlight for 7-10 days or until crumbly.
Steam distill the dried thymus vulgaris leaves with water using a copper alembic at 100°c for 30 minutes.
Collect and filter the essential oil through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, discarding any excess plant material.
Store the thymus vulgaris essential oil in an airtight container away from heat sources and direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat tinnitus with herbal essential oils is to use a combination of Lavender, Clary Sage, and Rosemary oils. These oils have anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic properties that help reduce stress and promote relaxation, which are known to alleviate tinnitus symptoms. Add 5-7 drops of each oil to a diffuser or mix with a carrier oil for topical application. Regular use has been shown to improve sleep quality and reduce tinnitus severity.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to tinnitus that are treated with herbal essential oils are earaches, ear infections, and vertigo. Essential oils like lavender, peppermint, and eucalyptus have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe earaches and infections, while also promoting relaxation and reducing stress, which can exacerbate tinnitus-like symptoms of vertigo. These herbal essential oils can be used topically or aromatically to provide relief from these conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with tinnitus because of its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the ear and surrounding tissues. The oil's warming and analgesic effects also help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with tinnitus. Furthermore, the oil's ability to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress may contribute to its benefits in addressing tinnitus symptoms. remedy_constituents: Beta-sesquiphellandrene: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the ear and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the ear from damage and reduce the severity of tinnitus.
Germacrene D: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the ear, contributing to relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale for essential oil production due to their high concentration of zingiberene, a compound believed to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Buds: Buds are used to make essential oils for tinnitus because they contain higher levels of certain sesquiterpenes that may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the ear.
Roots: Roots of Zingiber officinale are also used for essential oil production, as they contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature zingiber officinale rhizomes when the foliage begins to yellow and die back naturally.
Wash the harvested rhizomes with cold running water to remove dirt and impurities thoroughly.
Dry the washed rhizomes in a warm dry place with good air circulation for several days.
Steam distill the dried rhizomes for 2-3 hours at a temperature of 100-110 degrees celsius carefully.
Filter the resulting distillate using a coffee filter paper to obtain the essential oil slowly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with tinnitus because of its calming and soothing properties, which can reduce stress and anxiety that often exacerbate the condition. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds may also help alleviate ear inflammation, a common underlying cause of tinnitus. Additionally, Lavandula angustifolia has been found to improve sleep quality, which is essential for tinnitus management, as sleep disturbances can worsen symptoms. remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the ear and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Linalyl acetate: This ester terpene has sedative and anxiolytic effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may contribute to tinnitus.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the ear from oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used for essential oils because of their high concentration of lavender oil, which is known for its calming and soothing properties.

Leaves: They are used for essential oils because they contain a higher percentage of camphor, which is said to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Stems: They are used for essential oils because they contain a higher percentage of linalool, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that may help reduce tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Steam distill the dried flowers using 2 parts flowers to 1 part water for 1-2 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any remaining impurities and achieve a clear oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with tinnitus because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's ability to soothe and calm the nervous system may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, Melissa's sedative properties can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are often associated with tinnitus. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Melissa officinalis essential oils may provide relief from tinnitus symptoms and improve overall ear health.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Limonene: This terpene has neuroprotective effects, which can help protect the auditory nerve and reduce tinnitus-related nerve damage.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the auditory system, alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melissa officinalis are commonly used to make essential oils for tinnitus due to their high content of citral, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties.

Flowers: The flowers are often used due to their high concentration of citral and linalool, which have been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Stems: The stems of Melissa officinalis are occasionally used to make essential oils for tinnitus as they contain a lower concentration of citral and linalool compared to the leaves and flowers but still offer some therapeutic benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis flowers in the morning to obtain the best quality for distillation.
Dry the freshly harvested flowers by spreading them out in a single layer for 7 to 10 days.
Set up a steam distillation apparatus to separate the essential oil from the plant material.
Distill the dried melissa officinalis flowers using a steam distillation process for 2 to 3 hours.
Collect and filter the essential oil to remove any impurities and ensure quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with tinnitus because it improves blood circulation to the ears, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy auditory environment. The antioxidants in Ginkgo biloba oil help protect the delicate hair cells in the inner ear from damage, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and promote healing. By addressing the underlying causes of tinnitus, Ginkgo biloba essential oils can help alleviate symptoms, such as ringing, buzzing, and other unpleasant sounds, and promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate tinnitus by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the auditory system, thereby improving blood flow and reducing damage to the hair cells in the ear.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba oil have anti-inflammatory properties that help in reducing the inflammation in the auditory nerve, which is often associated with tinnitus, and promote a healthy auditory environment.

Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone compound is known to have neuroprotective effects, which help in reducing the damage to the hair cells in the ear and promoting the regeneration of new hair cells, thus helping to alleviate tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are commonly used to create essential oils due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for tinnitus treatment.

Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are another commonly used part in essential oil production, as they contain a compound called bilobalide, which has been shown to have neuroprotective effects and may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Buds: The buds of Ginkgo biloba are also used in essential oil production, as they contain a high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to the plant's potential therapeutic benefits for tinnitus treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 kg of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in the fall season when they are fully ripe.
Dry the leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill 1 kg of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 3 kg of water at 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separating funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils helps with tinnitus because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate the condition. remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It helps with tinnitus by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can alleviate symptoms of tinnitus.
Isovaleric acid: It helps with tinnitus by acting as a natural anxiolytic and sedative, thereby reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms.
Bornyl acetate: It helps with tinnitus by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can protect the inner ear from damage and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are rich in valerenic acid, a compound that is believed to have sedative and anti-anxiety properties, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Stems: The stems of Valeriana officinalis also contain valerenic acid and are sometimes used in combination with roots or leaves to create a more balanced essential oil.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature roots of valeriana officinalis in the early morning hours after the dew has evaporated naturally.
Wash the harvested roots thoroughly with lukewarm water to remove dirt and debris from the plant surface.
Steam distill the cleaned roots at a temperature range of 100-150°f to extract the essential oil.
Collect the extracted essential oil from the distillation apparatus and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the final product in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it with the date and contents.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oils helps with tinnitus because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate the condition. The oil's sedative properties can also help regulate the body's internal rhythms, promoting relaxation and reducing the likelihood of tinnitus episodes. remedy_constituents: Isoschaftoside: Acts as an antioxidant to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development and progression of tinnitus.
Flavonoids: Have anti-inflammatory properties to reduce inflammation in the auditory system, which can be a contributing factor to tinnitus.
Ferulic acid: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to protect the auditory system from damage and reduce the severity of tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Passiflora incarnata are commonly used to make essential oils to treat tinnitus due to their high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds.
Fruits: The fruits of Passiflora incarnata are also used to make essential oils, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Flowers: The flowers of Passiflora incarnata are used to make essential oils, which contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help reduce tinnitus symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the aerial parts of the passiflora incarnata plant in the morning after the dew has dried completely.
Dry the harvested aerial parts in a well-ventilated area at 35-40°c for 7-10 days.
Use a steam distillation apparatus to distill 100g of dried aerial parts with 200ml of distilled water for 2 hours.
Collect the obtained essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter to remove any impurities.
Store the passiflora incarnata essential oil in a dark glass bottle at a temperature below 30°c for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with tinnitus because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with this condition. The oil's ability to improve blood circulation and reduce anxiety may also contribute to its potential benefits in managing tinnitus. Additionally, rosmarinic acid, a compound found in rosemary essential oil, has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms. This may make it a useful natural remedy for those seeking alternative treatments.
remedy_constituents: Bornyl acetate: Helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with tinnitus by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties.
Cineole: Exhibits neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce tinnitus severity by protecting the auditory nerve from damage and reducing inflammation.
Rosmarinic acid: Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to tinnitus development and progression.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of antioxidants and essential oil compounds that help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
Flowers: Rosmarinus officinalis flowers are used to create essential oils that promote relaxation and reduce stress, which are often contributing factors to tinnitus.
Stems: Rosmarinus officinalis stems are used to produce essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms caused by inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves when they are in full bloom and the temperature is warm.
Steam distill the leaves for 2-3 hours using a copper still and 2l of distilled water.
Collect 50ml of the distilled essential oil from the distillation process after 2-3 hours.
Filter the essential oil using a paper filter to remove any impurities or debris.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to 1 year.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with tinnitus because it has potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and irritation in the ear canal. The oil's antimicrobial properties also help to combat infections that may be contributing to tinnitus. Furthermore, Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils contain compounds that have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress, which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms. As a result, many people have found relief from tinnitus symptoms after using Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the ear, contributing to the alleviation of tinnitus symptoms.
Linalool: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce the inflammation associated with tinnitus, leading to a decrease in its severity.
Terpinen-4-ol: Displays antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that can help protect the ear from damage and promote the recovery from tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part to make essential oils for tinnitus due to their high concentration of compounds with potential therapeutic benefits.
Barks: The barks of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to make essential oils that may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to make essential oils that may help with tinnitus as they contain compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with tinnitus because its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties can alleviate ear congestion and inflammation, which are common underlying causes of tinnitus. The oil's natural expectorant properties also help to clear mucus from the Eustachian tube, promoting a healthy ear environment. Additionally, eucalyptus oil's analgesic and anxiolytic properties can help to reduce stress and discomfort associated with tinnitus, promoting a sense of calm and well-being. This can lead to improved hearing and reduced tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Cineole has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
Limonene: Limonene has antioxidant properties that help protect the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the development of tinnitus.
α-Pinene: α-Pinene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high content of eucalyptol, a primary component responsible for the therapeutic properties of Eucalyptus globulus essential oil.

Barks: The barks of Eucalyptus globulus contain a high concentration of eucalyptol and other bioactive compounds, which contribute to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for tinnitus treatment.
Stems: The stems of Eucalyptus globulus also contain eucalyptol and other beneficial compounds, making them a viable source for essential oil production to help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 kg of mature eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning to avoid heat stress.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at 40-60°c for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separation funnel.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 10-micron filter to remove impurities and improve clarity.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle at a temperature below 25°c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils helps with tinnitus because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and damage to the hair cells in the ear responsible for sound processing. The oil's ability to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress may also contribute to its potential benefits. Additionally, Cinnamomum verum contains compounds that may have a soothing effect on the nervous system, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms such as ringing or buzzing in the ears.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation in the ear canal and surrounding tissues that may be contributing to tinnitus.
Cinnamaldehyde: Exhibits antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals that can damage the hair cells in the inner ear and lead to tinnitus.
Cinnamyl acetate: Possesses analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in the ear that may be exacerbating tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cinnamomum verum are commonly used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of eugenol, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Buds: Cinnamomum verum buds are also used to produce essential oils for tinnitus relief as they contain eugenol and linalool, compounds that have been shown to have pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Cinnamomum verum are used to create essential oils that may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms due to their high concentration of cinnamaldehyde, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean 2 kg of fresh cinnamomum verum leaves and twigs, rinsing them with water to remove any dirt.
Steam distill the harvested material for 2-3 hours, using a steam distillation apparatus to separate the oil from water.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from water using a separatory funnel, or by decanting the oil into a glass container.
Filter the collected oil through a coffee filter or a paper filter to remove any impurities or debris.
Store the final product in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, protecting it from light and heat.
